title: hieracium cirritum subsp. hypochaerideum
intro: Hieracium cirritum subsp. hypochaerideum, also known as False Hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The herbal preparations of Hieracium cirritum subsp. hypochaerideum often involve infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, which can be used to create teas, salves, or topical applications for various therapeutic purposes.
